title: pouteria surumuensis
intro: Pouteria surumuensis, also known as the Surumu tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Pouteria surumuensis plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Pouteria surumuensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or used in traditional medicine to create remedies for a range of health conditions.
